name: Xbalanque
description: Xbalanque, meaning 'Jaguar Sun' or 'Hidden Jaguar', is one of the Hero Twins in Mayan mythology, alongside his brother Hunahpu. Born after their father, Hun Hunahpu, was sacrificed in Xibalba, the Mayan underworld, Xbalanque and Hunahpu avenged their father and uncle, Seven Hunahpu, by outsmarting and defeating the Lords of Xibalba. Xbalanque is often depicted as a jaguar and is associated with the sun, warfare, and the cyclical nature of life and death. He possesses immense strength, cunning, and magical abilities, including the power to transform and resurrect. Xbalanque's journey through Xibalba represents the struggle between good and evil, light and darkness, and the triumph of life over death. He is a symbol of resilience, cleverness, and the enduring power of the human spirit. His defeat of the underworld lords paved the way for the creation of the present world and humankind. Xbalanque is considered a patron deity of warriors, hunters, and scribes, embodying courage, skill, and wisdom. He is a significant figure in the Popol Vuh, the Mayan creation story, and his exploits continue to inspire and resonate within Mayan culture.
subtitle: Jaguar Sun, Hero Twin, Avenger

keyMyths.title: Descent into Xibalba
keyMyths.description: Xbalanque and Hunahpu descend into Xibalba to avenge their father and uncle. They face numerous trials and tribulations set by the Lords of the Underworld.

keyMyths.title: The Ballgame
keyMyths.description: The Hero Twins challenge the Lords of Xibalba to a ballgame. Using their cunning and skill, they overcome the challenges and defeat their opponents.

keyMyths.title: Defeat of Vucub Caquix
keyMyths.description: Before their descent, the Hero Twins defeat Vucub Caquix, a false sun god who claimed to be the source of light and power.

sources.author: Dennis Tedlock
sources.description: A modern translation and interpretation of the Popol Vuh.
sources.text: A modern translation and interpretation of the Popol Vuh.
